items.title: Did Dyche deserve the sack at Nottingham Forest?
items.summary: After Sean Dyche's reign lasts just 114 days, Nottingham Forest are on the hunt for a fourth head coach of the season - but as he hard done by?

items.title: Ratcliffe 'sorry' for language in immigration claims
items.summary: Manchester United co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe has said he is sorry if he has "offended some people" by saying the United Kingdom has been "colonised by immigrants."

items.title: Dyche out as Forest sack 3rd coach this season
items.summary: Nottingham Forest sacked coach Sean Dyche on Wednesday, following his team's 0-0 draw with Premier League bottom-club Wolves, the team announced.

items.title: Haaland gives Man City injury scare after HT sub
items.summary: Erling Haaland has given Manchester City an injury scare after Pep Guardiola revealed the striker asked to be substituted during the 3-0 win over Fulham.

items.title: After 2-year delay, UEFA ExCo to include FIFPRO
items.summary: The Europe's players' union is set to be included with voting right on UEFA's all-powerful executive committee on Feb. 12 during the European governing body's annual congress on Feb. 12.

items.title: Witte zakdoekjes bij Eagles: 'Maar Boel is ook totaal niet geholpen'
items.summary: Melvin Boel staat er bij de fans van Go Ahead Eagles niet goed op en na de nederlaag in de Adelaarshorst tegen Heerenveen werd er om zijn ontslag geroepen. Clubwatcher Sander Janssen en hoofdredacteur Pieter Zwart vinden dat niet helemaal terecht, zo wordt uitgelegd in deze video.

items.title: Pepi op de weg terug bij PSV: 'Hij mag weer in de duels'
items.summary: PSV opent het Eredivisie-weekend vrijdag met een uitwedstrijd tegen FC Volendam. Ricardo Pepi is er in het vissersdorp nog niet bij, maar de aanvaller nadert zijn rentree. 'Op de trainingen hoeft hij niet alleen meer met afwerken bezig te zijn.'

items.title: Bring on the old guard to beat the drop: can Ange’s recall be right twist for Spurs? | Max Rushden
items.summary: If Tottenham are waiting for Pochettino part two, then season three of Postecoglou might bring the right survival vibes It’s panic time at the bottom of the Premier League, and if the past couple of days are anything to go by, probably don’t go following Ange Postecoglou into a job any time soon. Others who’ve followed it more closely can do Nottingham Forest and their 4 (four) managers. This is a piece about Tottenham Hotspur, or as I like to call them, my big team who win things. November 2023 feels like a lifetime ago. Spurs were top of the league. Angeball was at its peak. Dynamic free-flowing football – they were 1-0 up against Chelsea thanks to Dejan Kulusevski (injured). It’s the 14th minute, Spurs neatly play themselves out from back down the right, it breaks to Pape Sarr who rolls the ball to Destiny Udogie (injured), and Brennan Johnson (Crystal Palace) steams down the left. He plays a perfect first-time ball with his left foot into the path of Son Heung-min (LAFC), who rolls it home. Tottenham are 2-0 up against a team they lose to at least twice a season. Continue reading...

items.title: Opluchting groot bij FC Utrecht: 'Het moest niet lang meer duren'
items.summary: FC Utrecht weet weer wat winnen is. De ploeg van trainer Ron Jans zakte de afgelopen weken steeds verder weg in de Eredivisie, maar was woensdag verrassend te sterk voor NEC. Het plan van Utrecht pakte perfect uit en leverde de eerste Eredivisie-zege sinds 9 november 2025 op. Na afloop was de opluchting dan ook groot.

items.title: Saibari krijgt vrije dagen van Bosz: 'De frisheid neemt af'
items.summary: Peter Bosz heeft Ismael Saibari een paar dagen vrij gegeven. De trainer van PSV merkte dat de frisheid afnam bij de Marokkaanse middenvelder, die geen winterstop had door zijn Afrika Cup-deelname. Saibari mag de uitwedstrijd van vrijdag tegen FC Volendam daarom overslaan.

items.title: Tottenham are 'not a big club' - Postecoglou
items.summary: Former head coach Ange Postecoglou criticises Tottenham's transfer strategy in the wake of Thomas Frank's sacking.

items.title: Maloney expects more late drama in title race | Martinez to Celtic?
items.summary: It is the Scottish Premiership title race that refuses to follow the same old script - and Celtic assistant Shaun Maloney expects there to be plenty more twists in the tale before May.

items.title: Van 16-jarig jochie tot modelprof: recordhouder Milner komt uit een andere tijd
items.summary: Feyenoord had een half jaar eerder de UEFA Cup gewonnen, Ronaldo en Zinedine Zidane waren nog de sterspelers van Real Madrid en Arne Slot was net een paar maanden speler van NAC Breda. Zolang is het geleden dat James Milner zijn Premier League-debuut maakte. Ruim 23 jaar later vestigde de Engelse middenvelder een record.

items.title: FA to look at whether Jim Ratcliffe broke football rules by saying UK ‘colonised’ by immigrants
items.summary: FA assessing whether he brought game into disrepute Manchester United co-owner told by Starmer to apologise The Football Association will look at whether Jim Ratcliffe brought the game into disrepute through his claims that the UK has been “colonised” by immigrants. The Manchester United co-owner, a billionaire based in Monaco, caused widespread anger with his comments and has been called on by Keir Starmer to apologise. His remarks have not gone unnoticed within the FA, which will examine further before deciding whether he has broken any rules. It is too early to say whether any formal investigation will follow. Continue reading...

items.title: Two children, no sleep, 'carnage' - Andrew boxes on same bill as husband
items.summary: “I get no sleep.” Two small children will do that to any parent. But factor into that as well training for a boxing match, promoting your first show and making a bit of boxing history by competing alongside your husband in the same event.

items.title: Unbridled: Should Henderson go public with Constitution Hill's schooling?
items.summary: Matt Chapman has called on Nicky Henderson to make Constitution Hill’s schooling videos public to act as 'back-up' if they decide to run in the Champion Hurdle, despite concerns over the horse’s recent falls.

items.title: Tuchel signs new deal to lead England until 2028
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel has signed a contract extension to keep him as England head coach through to the end of Euro 2028.

items.title: Thomas Tuchel signs England contract extension to stay as head coach until 2028
items.summary: Head coach’s deal was due to finish after World Cup Tuchel keen to lead team at Euros co-hosted by England Thomas Tuchel has signed an extension to his England contract that will keep him in charge of the national team until after Euro 2028. The fresh deal means Tuchel, who took over in January 2025, will remain manager regardless of England’s fate at the World Cup this summer. He originally signed an 18-month deal but will now be at the helm for a home European Championship in two-and-a-half years’ time. Continue reading...

items.title: 'I didn't hesitate' - Tuchel extends stay in England 'dream job'
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel commits to two more years as England manager, which will take him through to Euro 2028.

items.title: Lucky break that changed Alexander-Arnold's Real future
items.summary: Trent Alexander-Arnold endured a difficult start at Real Madrid but his prospects are looking up - and it might earn him a ticket to the World Cup.
